adds.rationale: Rocket Lab is the only publicly-traded pure-play small-launch company with an active and growing orbital cadence — two successful Electron missions in Q2 2022 alone — making it the most direct early-buildup entry into the rockets-and-spacecraft wave the profile explicitly names as a likely next wave.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab's 26th Electron mission 'There And Back Again' on May 2, 2022 delivers 34 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit and makes history with the first-ever helicopter mid-air booster-capture attempt, demonstrating growing launch cadence and early reusability progress.

Rocket Lab's 25th Electron mission carries two BlackSky Earth-observation satellites to orbit in April 2022, the company's first of two successful commercial missions in the quarter, continuing a multi-year track record of reliable small-satellite delivery.

adds.rationale: The Global X Robotics and AI ETF (launched 2016, highly liquid) provides diversified exposure to the robotics wave the profile names as a next wave, and Amazon's June 22, 2022 unveiling of its first fully autonomous mobile warehouse robot confirms the wave has entered a clear commercial-deployment buildup phase driven by persistent labor shortages.

news_evidence.summary: Amazon unveils 'Proteus,' its first fully autonomous mobile warehouse robot, at the Re:Mars conference on June 22, 2022, designed to navigate freely around workers and automate GoCart transport, signaling mainstream commercial adoption of autonomous robotics to offset labor shortages.

Amazon simultaneously reveals Cardinal, an AI-powered robotic arm for package sorting, alongside Proteus at Re:Mars, reinforcing the breadth of its 2022 warehouse-automation push and reflecting industrywide investment in robotics to address the ongoing U.S. labor shortage.

adds.rationale: Russia's ongoing invasion of Ukraine has triggered a structural, multi-year NATO rearmament cycle cemented at the June 2022 Madrid summit, making the iShares U.S. Aerospace and Defense ETF a direct and liquid way to capture the geopolitical-realignment wave the profile watches, without single-stock concentration risk.

news_evidence.summary: Foreign Policy reports on June 30, 2022 that Putin's Ukraine invasion has 'turbocharged' NATO defense spending, with alliance members at the Madrid summit committing to new strategic concepts, higher force posture targets, and multi-year budget increases well above the 2-percent GDP floor.

PBS NewsHour reports NATO Secretary-General Stoltenberg calling for larger defense budgets and a harder spending target as the Madrid summit formalizes that the Ukraine war has permanently reshaped the alliance's defense posture.

rationale_overall: The existing five-ticker watchlist (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, SPY) is concentrated entirely in the AI and broad-market buckets with zero coverage of the next-wave themes the profile identifies as buildup-stage targets. This first rebalance fills all three open slots to reach the 8-ticker cap: RKLB captures the rockets-and-spacecraft wave via the only publicly-traded pure-play small-launch operator, which is actively executing commercial missions in Q2 2022; BOTZ adds diversified robotics exposure as Amazon's Proteus announcement confirms the wave has entered commercial deployment; and ITA captures the structural defense-rearmament wave that the NATO Madrid summit has made explicit as a multi-decade commitment. All five existing tickers are retained — their shared drawdown in the 2022 rate-hike bear market is a multiple compression story, not a thesis breach — and the watchlist now spans five distinct wave buckets rather than one.
